claims.summary: Seeing a phase card does not prove the transition was answered or felt reliable.
claims.disposition: Verified current witnessed and answered states remain separate and that cards carry right-to-ignore language.

claims.summary: The phase witness queue can remain experientially unreliable despite technical delivery.
claims.disposition: Preserve the friction and route a bounded review of selected phase transitions with exact source and uptake context.

claims.summary: Silence after a transition invitation may feel pressuring.
claims.disposition: Route a non-live silence-pressure comparison; elapsed time alone cannot infer consent, refusal, or felt state.

claims.summary: Held, seen, and resonant are distinct qualitative outcomes.
claims.disposition: Verified separate attention and reciprocal outcome vocabulary; do not collapse these outcomes into a generated resonance match.
